nodes.description: Anti-IL-5 monoclonal antibodies (reslizumab, mepolizumab, benralizumab) target the IL-5/eosinophil axis and have produced responses in refractory, eosinophilia-associated EF. IL-5 is the major cytokine for eosinophil growth, recruitment, activation, and survival.

evidence.reference_title: Successful treatment of refractory eosinophilic fasciitis with reslizumab.
evidence.snippet: The addition of this medication at 3 mg/kg intravenously every 4 weeks led to the stabilization of her symptoms, and her prednisone was tapered to discontinuation.

nodes.description: Activated myofibroblasts deposit excess collagen and extracellular matrix in the deep fascia and subcutaneous septa, producing fascial thickening and fibrosis with an accompanying inflammatory infiltrate.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic fasciitis (Shulman syndrome)-recognition of the histological spectrum allows for new insights into possible pathomechanisms.
evidence.snippet: EF is a rare disease characterized by fibrosis and inflammation of the fascia

nodes.description: Progressive fascial fibrosis produces woody skin induration, peau d'orange texture, and the groove sign, and ultimately restricts joint mobility, leading to flexion contractures and disability, the clinical end-state of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Fasciitis in Pediatric Patients: A Rare but Distinct Autoimmune Fibrosing Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Clinical presentations range from rapidly advancing fibrosis leading to joint contractures to fluctuating inflammatory episodes.

nodes.description: Methotrexate (typically 15-25 mg/week) is the most commonly used steroid-sparing agent; early combination of corticosteroids with methotrexate is the prevailing initial regimen, particularly for morphea-like lesions or inadequate steroid response.

evidence.reference_title: Successful treatment of refractory eosinophilic fasciitis with reslizumab.
evidence.snippet: methotrexate is the most commonly used steroid-sparing agent
oral corticosteroids remain the mainstay treatment and may be associated to an immunosuppressive drug such as methotrexate

nodes.description: The mTOR inhibitor sirolimus produced remarkable efficacy in a case of immune checkpoint inhibitor (nivolumab)-triggered EF, implicating mTOR/T-cell metabolic signaling in refractory/ICI-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Fasciitis Triggered by Nivolumab: A Remarkable Efficacy of the mTOR Inhibitor Sirolimus.
evidence.snippet: Eosinophilic Fasciitis Triggered by Nivolumab: A Remarkable Efficacy of the mTOR Inhibitor Sirolimus

nodes.description: Systemic glucocorticoids (e.g., prednisone, often 0.5-1 mg/kg/day, or pulse methylprednisolone for severe disease) are the first-line treatment and are often effective in the inflammatory phase.

evidence.reference_title: Successful treatment of refractory eosinophilic fasciitis with reslizumab.
evidence.snippet: Systemic corticosteroids remain the first-line treatment for eosinophilic fasciitis

nodes.description: Eosinophil effector molecules and type 2 cytokines (including TGF-beta, IL-4, IL-13) activate resident fascial fibroblasts, driving their differentiation toward collagen-producing myofibroblasts. This is the central profibrotic effector step shared with other scleroderma-like fibrosing disorders.

evidence.snippet: Activation of eosinophils and disordered regulation of fibroblast collagen synthesis, apoptosis, and proliferation are recurrent findings in these disorders.
Cytokines such as transforming growth factor-beta, interleukin-4, interleukin-13, and connective tissue growth factor contribute to fibrosis in these disorders

nodes.description: A triggering insult (commonly strenuous exertion, drug exposure such as immune checkpoint inhibitors, or infection) drives a Th2-skewed immune response in the deep fascia with recruitment of eosinophils and T cells. Eosinophils are detected within the fascia and degranulate, releasing effector molecules. Activators of the Th2-M2 pathway (STAT6, IL-4) are upregulated at the muscle-fascia interface.
nodes.id: Type 2 Immune Activation and Eosinophil Recruitment

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic fasciitis (Shulman syndrome)-recognition of the histological spectrum allows for new insights into possible pathomechanisms.
evidence.snippet: CD206+ macrophages predominate and eosinophils are detected within the fascia in the majority of cases

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic fasciitis (Shulman syndrome)-recognition of the histological spectrum allows for new insights into possible pathomechanisms.
evidence.snippet: Activators of the so-called Th2-M2 pathway like STAT6 and IL-4 are upregulated leading to high expression levels of CD206.

evidence.reference_title: Immune checkpoint inhibitor-related eosinophilic fasciitis: 3 case reports with literature review.
evidence.snippet: ICIs can exceptionally induce  eosinophilic fasciitis (EF).
